title: Crispy Bitter Greek Pork Shoulder Roast
description: A lazy‑Sunday Greek-inspired pork shoulder roast with a crisp, bitter citrus crust, baked to perfection and diabetic‑friendly.

ingredients.name: Pork shoulder (bone‑in, trimmed of excess fat)

instructions: Preheat the oven to 250 °C (482 °F) and set the broiler to high.
Pat the pork shoulder dry with paper towels; this helps the crust form.
In a small bowl, whisk together olive oil, lemon juice, oregano, rosemary, garlic powder, salt, and pepper.
Rub the mixture all over the pork, pressing it into the surface.
Place the pork on a wire rack over a rimmed baking sheet and roast for 45 minutes, turning once, until the internal temperature reaches 56 °C (133 °F).
Remove from the oven, cover loosely with foil, and let rest for 10 minutes.
Increase the oven temperature to 260 °C (500 °F) and switch the oven to broil mode.
Return the pork to the oven and broil for 3–5 minutes, watching closely, until the exterior is a deep golden brown and crisp.
Turn off the heat, slice the pork against the grain, and serve immediately with a handful of bitter greens drizzled with a splash of olive oil and a squeeze of fresh lemon.
Optionally, add a small sprinkle of grated Parmesan cheese for extra flavor (omit for strict lactose‑free).
